treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Partial resection due to severe adhesion to the spinal cord and nerve roots
treatment_information.treatment_response: Immediately after the operation, the patient’s paraplegia did not change. Gradual recovery from lower-extremity weakness was observed postoperatively.

treatment_timeline.event: Patient presented with weakness in both ankles and toes since birth.
Underwent T-12 to L-4 laminoplastic laminotomy.
MRI showed residual tumor extending from the lower thoracic to the sacral level.
Follow-up MRI revealed an unchanged residual lesion with decreased enhancement.
Patient showed improved motor power in the lower extremities (up to MRC Grade 3/5).

patient_outcome.final_status: Improved motor power in the lower extremities (up to MRC Grade 3/5), but could not walk and had poor control of urinary and fecal functions.
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Follow-up MR images obtained 19 months after surgery and clinical follow-up at 38 months post-operation.
patient_outcome.long_term_prognosis: Residual tumor remained unchanged with decreased enhancement. Gradual recovery observed.
patient_outcome.recovery_timeline: Gradual improvement in motor power observed over 38 months.
patient_outcome.quality_of_life: Patient could not walk and had poor control of urinary and fecal functions.
